THE COURT WOULD NOT ENTER JUDGMENT TO ALLOW PLAINTIFF TO AMEND HER COMPLAINT TO ADD A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY UNTIL FEBRUARY 2, 2012. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-07-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JOHNNIE L. BROWN, TEL. NO. 437-1423, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. MICHAEL A. GERCHOW, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3936 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant produces prima facie evidence that the alleged dangerous condition was not caused by a negligent, or wrongful act or omission by its employees within the scope of employment, or that Defendant had actual or constructive notice of the dangerous condition. (Gov. Code 835; See also Declaration of Gerchow in Support of Motion, Exh. D, Nos. 25-27; Declaration of Popp in Support of Motion, 2, 5-6; Declaration of Hao in Support of Motion, 3-4, Exhs. B, C.) Consequently the burden shifts to Plaintiff. Plaintiff has not demonstrated a triable issue of material facts exists. Plaintiff relies on inadmissible evidence to dispute the material facts. (See Declaration of Brown in Support of Opposition, 11; Declaration of Pipkin in Support of Opposition, 8.) "Evidence that gives rise to no more than mere speculation is insufficient to establish a triable issue of material fact." (Sangster v. Paetkau (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 151, 163.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)
